domain: Browser-based educational block-coding puzzle game with telemetry-backed analytics
constraints: Frontend and backend implemented in Next.js 16.x with TypeScript.
Node v24.11.0 and npm 11.6.1 compatible dependencies only.
Persistence via SQLite using better-sqlite3; DB auto-creates on first run.
Block editor anchored by `On Start` root with left command library (movement, actions, control/loops, logic/conditionals, sensing) and right active code area; disconnected block warning required.
Run controls (`Play`, `Reset`, speed toggle) must be highly visible with no dark colors; workspace minimizes during execution.
Failure feedback must show `Oops!` plus hint covering reasons (target not reached, wrong item, wrong order, obstacle collision, missing condition).
Workspace assets use provided sprites/backgrounds/layout; align puzzle scenes with `design/layout_refs/layout.png` guidance.
Rendering must be polished cartoon WebGL-based 2D with shades of pink/violet, high contrast, clear actors/items/goals, and cohesive UI styling.
Block editor must be drag-and-drop (custom or heavily styled Blockly).
Telemetry must capture all gameplay/UI events including every movement and block execution step via HTTP APIs to backend.
success_criteria: Exactly 3 progression-linked puzzles covering sequencing, loops, and conditionals.
`Play` executes connected blocks, animates scene, and uses stored puzzle data; `Reset` restores state.
Incorrect solutions trigger `Oops!` plus contextual hint.
SQLite stores `users`, `sessions`, `puzzles`, `attempts`, `events`, `movements`, `puzzle_progress` with telemetry rows for runs and movement steps.
Analytics dashboard reads SQLite data, shows aggregates, puzzle detail, event streams, and can replay movement paths.
Show Code toggle renders read-only text version of current program.
Accessibility: keyboard navigation for main controls, adequate contrast, no color-only signals, optional text-to-speech for goal text.
expected_outputs: Complete Next.js app (frontend gameplay UI, backend APIs).
Puzzle data definitions enabling data-driven scenes (id, title, story, goal, scene id, grid, entities, blocks, constraints, success criteria, hints).
SQLite schema/init logic for telemetry tables.
Game assets arranged per provided layout reference and reusable scene composition.
stack_requirements: Next.js 16.x with TypeScript for both UI and server routes.
Drag-and-drop block system (custom or styled Blockly) integrated into Next.js frontend.
validation_expectations: Verify automatic SQLite initialization on first launch.
Test telemetry flow: session start/end, event batching, attempts, movement logging, code snapshots.
Confirm analytics endpoints/pages can read stored data and replay runs.
Exercise puzzles to ensure progression, failure feedback, and `Oops!` hints operate.
Ensure accessibility features (keyboard controls, contrast, text-to-speech) work.
repo_constraints: Use local assets in `public/assets/...` and layout reference in `design/layout_refs/layout.png`.
Front/back code plus data must live within repository; no external services or proprietary brands.
Art/style must remain original yet aligned with provided assets/layout.
missing_information: Exact puzzle narratives, goals, and block configurations beyond provided layout guidance.
Specific analytics metrics/visualization layouts beyond aggregates/detail/event stream/replay.
assumptions: Later levels extrapolate layout reference while maintaining asset ordering and spacing logic.
Placeholder assets maintain sizing/anchors if replacements arrive later.
